column3: The liquid crystals control light, but the panel still needs a separate light source.
column4:
Exam Trap

The crystals are not the light source; LCD hardware depends on backlighting.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze LCD DisplayPanels Source::Messer-v170

column3: OLED pixels emit their own light instead of relying on a separate backlight.
column4:
Memory Trick

The "LE" in OLED is the useful clue: the display technology emits light.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze OLED DisplayPanels Source::Messer-v170

column3: TN is associated with fast response, with weaker viewing angles as the tradeoff.
column4:
Exam Trap

The acronym recall is separate from selecting TN for a response-time scenario.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze TN DisplayPanels Source::Messer-v170

column3: IPS is associated with strong color representation.
column4:
Related Cards

See 3.1-B002 for an IPS selection scenario.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze IPS DisplayPanels Source::Messer-v170

column3: VA is commonly treated as a compromise between TN and IPS characteristics.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not assume every LCD panel type has the same response, color, and viewing-angle behavior.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze VA DisplayPanels Source::Messer-v170

column2: Mini LED is an LCD backlight approach that uses {{c1::smaller LEDs}} for finer lighting control.
column3: It improves backlight control, especially in dark areas, without changing the panel into OLED.
column4:
Exam Trap

Mini LED describes the backlight system; OLED describes self-emissive pixels.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze MiniLED Backlight Source::Messer-v170

column2: Pixel density is commonly measured as pixels per {{c1::inch}}.
column3: It relates the pixel count to the display's physical size.
column4:
Why

Resolution alone does not describe how tightly those pixels are packed.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.21-22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze PixelDensity DisplayAttributes Source::Messer-v170

column2: Display refresh rate is usually measured in {{c1::hertz}}.
column3: Refresh rate describes how often the image can update per second.
column4:
Why

Higher refresh rates are most useful when the displayed content changes quickly.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze RefreshRate DisplayAttributes Source::Messer-v170

column2: Color gamut describes the {{c1::range of colors}} a display can represent.
column3: Coverage of a particular gamut standard can matter for color-sensitive work.
column4:
Exam Trap

A larger screen or higher resolution does not automatically mean better color coverage.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze ColorGamut DisplayAttributes Source::Messer-v170

column2: Screen resolution describes the number of pixels {{c1::across and down}}, commonly written as width x height.
column3: Resolution is the pixel count; pixel density relates that count to the display's physical size.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not replace the core resolution concept with memorized resolution-name tables.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Cloze Resolution DisplayAttributes Source::Messer-v170
